text.title: Requirements Lifecycle Examples
text.summary: Password Reset requirement state history and CR-012 login timeout change request walkthrough.

section.title: State History Example: REQ-045 Password Reset

example.code: | Date | State Change | Trigger | |--------|-------------------------------|----------------------------------| | Jan 5 | (none) → Draft | Created from stakeholder interview | | Jan 8 | Draft → Proposed | BA review complete, ready for sign-off | | Jan 10 | Proposed → Approved | Stakeholder sign-off obtained | | Jan 25 | Approved → Implemented | Development complete, PR merged | | Feb 1 | Implemented → Verified | Acceptance testing passed, UAT sign-off |
example.note: Each state change recorded with date and trigger — provides full audit trail without searching email threads.

example.code:
Change Request: CR-012 — Modify Login Session Timeout Requestor: Product Owner Affected: REQ-030 (Session Management)
Current requirement: Session expires after 15 minutes of inactivity. Proposed change: Session expires after 60 minutes of inactivity. Reason: Users report being logged out mid-workflow; support tickets indicate 30% of password reset requests follow a session timeout.
Impact analysis: - Requirements: REQ-030 v1.0 → v2.0 required; REQ-031 (Security Policy) must be reviewed for compliance with session security standards. - Design: Session token TTL configuration in AuthService. - Development: Config change only, no new code. - Testing: TC-050, TC-051 require timeout value update. - Schedule: 2-day effort, no sprint impact. - Cost: No additional cost.
Decision: Approved with modification — 30 minutes (not requested 60 minutes). Security review rejected 60 minutes as non-compliant with policy.
Result: REQ-030 updated to v2.0 with 30-minute timeout. Version history entry added.
